entities.name: GRAIN SORGHUM
entities.description: The crop being studied for its response to phosphorus fertilizer.

entities.name: PHOSPHORUS
entities.description: The nutrient applied as fertilizer to study its residual effects on crop growth.

entities.name: VERTISOL
entities.description: The specific soil type, Typic Pellustert, characterized by low extractable phosphorus.

entities.name: SEMI-ARID ZONE
entities.description: The climatic region in India where the study was conducted.

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where the research was conducted.

entities.name: RAINFED CROPPING
entities.description: The cropping system where water is supplied solely by rainfall.

entities.name: FIELD EXPERIMENT
entities.description: The research approach used to study the response of sorghum to phosphorus over three years.

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The measure of productivity of the grain sorghum.

entities.name: PHOSPHORUS UPTAKE
entities.description: The amount of phosphorus absorbed by the sorghum plant from the soil.

entities.name: RAINFALL
entities.description: The variable weather factor during the cropping season from June to September.
